mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production signals an expansion in the supply side of the economy, leading to an increase in demand for stocks and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 5-day forward returns are not significantly higher than the baseline in 60% of triggered instances (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than the baseline in 60% of triggered instances (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in payroll employment signals an expansion in the labor market, leading to an increase in consumer spending and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher than the baseline in 60% of triggered instances (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in housing starts signals an expansion in the housing market, leading to an increase in demand for stocks and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than the baseline in 60% of triggered instances (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in industrial production and a 1-month increase in housing starts signals an expansion in the supply side of the economy and the housing market, leading to an increase in demand for stocks and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 5-day forward returns are not significantly higher than the baseline in 60% of triggered instances (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month decline in the federal funds rate and a 1-month increase in the CPI signals an easing monetary cycle and an increase in inflation expectations, leading to an increase in stock prices and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than the baseline in 60% of triggered instances (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in payroll employment and a 2-month increase in the unemployment rate signals an expansion in the labor market and a decrease in labor market slack, leading to an increase in consumer spending and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher than the baseline in 60% of triggered instances (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP signals an expansion in the demand side of the economy, leading to an increase in stock prices and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 5-day forward returns are not significantly higher than the baseline in 60% of triggered instances (t-test p < 0.05).
